title: [CI:DOCS] Github: Add workflow to monitor Cirrus-Cron builds
body:
The Cirrus-CI configuration for this repository is setup to execute test
builds on certain important release branches.  There is no built-in way
to monitor these for success or failure.  This commit adds a
Github-Actions Workflow to e-mail the podman-monitor list if any fail.
Otherwise it will take no action if everything is successful.

Note: This duplicates 99.999% of the same YAML used for the Buildah
repository.  The only changes were for the settings URL and
mentioning "skopeo" in a comment.  A similar workflow is also in use
on the Podman repository.

Signed-off-by: Chris Evich <cevich@redhat.com>

comments.body:
***NOTE:*** There's no way to test this before merging :face_with_head_bandage: 

The diff vs same job in buildah:

```diff
--- ../buildah/.github/workflows/check_cirrus_cron.yml  2021-08-03 15:07:22.777008822 -0400
+++ .github/workflows/check_cirrus_cron.yml     2021-08-04 10:03:25.485512733 -0400
@@ -13,7 +13,7 @@
     schedule:
         # N/B: This should correspond to a period slightly after
         # the last job finishes running.  See job defs. at:
-        # https://cirrus-ci.com/settings/repository/5138144844840960
+        # https://cirrus-ci.com/settings/repository/6706677464432640
         - cron:  '59 23 * * 1-5'
     # Debug: Allow triggering job manually in github-actions WebUI
     workflow_dispatch: {}
@@ -36,7 +36,7 @@
               with:
                   persist-credentials: false

-            # Avoid duplicating cron_failures.sh in buildah repo.
+            # Avoid duplicating cron_failures.sh in skopeo repo.
             - uses: actions/checkout@v2
               with:
                   repository: containers/podman
```
Okay, I've added the necessary secret env. vars. so this workflow can function.

First manual run: https://github.com/containers/skopeo/actions/runs/1099005317

(Note: no cirrus-cron jobs have actually executed yet)
